message:
fix(store,runtime): WATCH ABA detection via per-key modification counter

The WATCH implementation used content fingerprints (hashes) to detect
key modifications. This missed the ABA scenario where a key is modified
and then restored to its original value — the fingerprint matches but
Redis would abort the transaction.

Fix:
- Add modification_count field to Entry, incremented on every touch()
- Add Store::key_modification_count() method
- WATCH records both fingerprint AND modification count
- EXEC compares both: if either changed, transaction aborts
- This correctly detects ABA modifications per-key (not global)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
